name: D+Q 20-week fatigue adverse-event result
arm_details: Extended Data Table 1 reports the fatigue row with a blank control cell and 4 (13%) in the D+Q cell. The safety section also reports one D+Q participant withdrew from active intervention at week 9 because of fatigue and other nonspecific side effects.

effect.raw_text: Fatigue row in Extended Data Table 1: control cell blank; D+Q 4 (13%). Safety section: one D+Q participant withdrew from active intervention at week 9 because of fatigue and other nonspecific side effects.

analysis.effect_note: No comparative fatigue effect was calculated because the control cell is blank in a table restricted to events reported in more than one participant for each group.

event_specific_counts.interpretation: The source table leaves the control fatigue cell blank under a threshold for adverse events reported in more than one participant; this is not encoded as zero.
The source table reports fatigue in 4 D+Q participants, 13% of the arm.

zero_handling.rationale: The retained table does not state whether the blank control cell means zero events or a count below the table's reporting threshold.

provenance.note: Extended Data Table 1 fatigue row reports a blank control cell and D+Q 4 (13%); the footnote says results are adverse events reported in more than one participant for each group.

Safety paragraph reports one D+Q participant withdrew from active intervention at week 9 because of fatigue and other nonspecific side effects, and no serious or severe D+Q-related adverse events occurred.

statement: In the D+Q postmenopausal bone RCT publication table, fatigue was reported for 4 of 30 D+Q participants (13%); the matching control cell was blank and is not treated as a zero count.
caveats: The publication table does not provide an explicit control fatigue count; the blank control cell is preserved as zero/not-reported ambiguity.
The fatigue preferred-term row and the separate fatigue-related withdrawal narrative should not be double-counted.
The open-label design may affect subjective adverse-event reporting.
